content:
# IVX PROJECT SEPARATION — EXPO/REACT NATIVE ONLY, FINAL APK LINK NOW

MODE: EXECUTE NOW
PRIORITY: P0
GOAL: Complete the real IVX Expo/React Native application, generate the final production APK, publish a direct download link, and prevent any Kotlin project from being mixed into the IVX app.

IMPORTANT OWNER DECISION:

- IVX is Expo / React Native.
- Do not continue Kotlin development.
- Do not mix Kotlin files, dependencies, builds, routes, assets, configuration, or output into the Expo application.
- Do not create another app.
- Do not create another package name.
- Do not create another repository.
- Do not change the existing IVX product architecture.
- The final APK must contain the complete IVX Expo app.

==================================================
ITEM 1 — LOCK THE CANONICAL PROJECT
==================================================

The only active mobile application source is:

- `expo/`

The backend source remains:

- `backend/`

Mark these as canonical:

- Expo/React Native app: `expo/`
- Production backend: `backend/`
- Repository: `ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest`
- Production branch: `main`

Do not use the abandoned Kotlin folder for any build.

==================================================
ITEM 2 — QUARANTINE OR REMOVE KOTLIN
==================================================

Audit:

- `android-ivx-holdings/`
- every `.kt` file
- Gradle files belonging only to the abandoned Kotlin template
- Kotlin-specific application IDs
- Kotlin-specific build scripts
- Kotlin assets
- Kotlin-generated APKs
- Kotlin references in scripts or CI

Required result:

- Kotlin is not imported by Expo.
- Kotlin is not included in the production build.
- Kotlin is not referenced by package scripts.
- Kotlin is not referenced by GitHub Actions.
- Kotlin is not referenced by Render.
- Kotlin is not referenced by EAS.
- Kotlin is not presented as an IVX production app.

Delete the abandoned folder only after proving it is not required by Expo prebuild or the current Android build.

If deletion cannot be performed from the Rork workspace:

- remove it from all active scripts and build paths
- add it to an explicit abandoned-code exclusion
- provide the exact independent-checkout deletion command
- confirm production does not use it

==================================================
ITEM 3 — VERIFY THE FULL EXPO APP IS INCLUDED
==================================================

The final APK must include the complete IVX app, not a reduced demo or separate shell.

Verify these modules are present:

- Home
- Invest
- Market
- Portfolio
- Chat
- Profile
- CRM
- Owner Login
- Owner AI
- Live Work
- Members
- Investors
- Buyers
- Properties
- Reels
- Deal Detail
- View Deal
- Invest Now
- Notifications
- Attachments
- Settings
- Admin/Owner controls

Verify production API connections:

- `https://api.ivxholding.com`
- `https://chat.ivxholding.com`
- `https://ivxholding.com`

Do not package placeholder, mock, sandbox, localhost, LAN, tunnel, Rork proxy, or Kotlin endpoints.

==================================================
ITEM 4 — VERIFY CURRENT UI FIXES
==================================================

Confirm the final Expo bundle contains:

- three regular investment cards first on Home
- up to eight images per regular card
- horizontal image carousel
- Casa Rosario reel after the first three cards
- canonical Reels card in the Reels module
- self-adjusting reel height
- no bottom-navigation overlap
- no infinite Deal Detail loading
- View Deal working
- Invest Now working
- no old all-reels Home regression

Return the exact files and tests proving this.

==================================================
ITEM 5 — CLEAN EXPO PRODUCTION CONFIGURATION
==================================================

Audit:

- `app.json`
- `app.config.*`
- `eas.json`
- Expo project ID
- Android package name
- app version
- versionCode
- update channel
- production API URL
- assets
- permissions
- signing configuration

Required:

- one IVX Android package
- one IVX Expo project
- one production build profile
- no Kotlin project identity
- no duplicate app
- no debug banner
- no development menu
- no localhost URL
- no secret in `EXPO_PUBLIC_*`
- no Rork runtime dependency
- no stale bundle

==================================================
ITEM 6 — CLEAN INSTALL AND FULL TEST
==================================================

Run from a clean Expo environment:

1. Locked dependency install.
2. Expo doctor.
3. Expo config validation.
4. Lint.
5. Typecheck.
6. Unit tests.
7. Integration tests.
8. Feed-order tests.
9. Navigation tests.
10. Owner-login tests.
11. Owner AI tests.
12. Attachment tests.
13. Production Android build.

For each command return:

- exact command
- working directory
- exit code
- result

Do not call the build successful if the complete Expo app does not compile.

==================================================
ITEM 7 — BUILD THE FINAL APK
==================================================

Generate one signed production APK from the `expo/` application.

Required:

- filename: `ivx-holdings-v<version>-build<versionCode>-final.apk`
- production environment
- latest GitHub SHA
- correct package name
- signed APK
- no Kotlin app
- no debug bundle
- complete IVX modules
- checksum generated

Return:

- APK filename
- direct HTTPS download URL
- file size
- SHA-256
- app version
- versionCode
- build ID
- build timestamp
- GitHub SHA used

The owner must be able to tap the URL on Android and download the APK directly.

Do not return only a local path such as:

`expo/android/app/build/outputs/...`

A local sandbox path is not a usable owner download link.

==================================================
ITEM 8 — PUBLISH THE APK
==================================================

Publish the APK to one owner-accessible location:

Preferred order:

1. GitHub Release in `ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest`
2. Owner-controlled storage/CDN
3. EAS build artifact with an accessible APK URL

Create a stable release tag such as:

`v<version>-build<versionCode>`

Verify the download URL:

- HTTP 200
- APK content type or downloadable binary
- file size matches
- SHA-256 matches
- no authentication loop
- no expired temporary URL during testing

==================================================
ITEM 9 — REAL DEVICE RELEASE CHECKLIST
==================================================

Provide the owner with the direct APK link and this concise checklist:

1. Download.
2. Allow installation from the browser when prompted.
3. Install.
4. Open IVX.
5. Sign in as owner.
6. Open Home.
7. Verify three regular cards.
8. Open Casa Rosario reel.
9. Open Reels.
10. Test View Deal.
11. Test Invest Now.
12. Open Owner AI.
13. Send one message.
14. Upload one image.
15. Close and reopen the app.
16. Confirm session and messages persist.

Do not claim physical-device verification until this is completed on the owner’s phone.

==================================================
ITEM 10 — FINAL PROJECT-SEPARATION PROOF
==================================================

Return a table:

| Area | Canonical source | Kotlin involved? | Result |
| Mobile app | expo/ | NO | PASS/FAIL |
| Backend | backend/ | NO | PASS/FAIL |
| APK build | Expo/React Native | NO | PASS/FAIL |
| Package name | [actual value] | NO | PASS/FAIL |
| GitHub release | [tag] | NO | PASS/FAIL |
| Production API | api.ivxholding.com | NO | PASS/FAIL |

Also return:

- all Kotlin references found
- all Kotlin references removed or excluded
- all Expo files included in the build
- final project tree summary
- build source path
- GitHub SHA
- APK link

==================================================
MANDATORY FINAL RESPONSE
==================================================

PROJECT:
- Canonical mobile project:
- Canonical backend:
- Repository:
- Branch:

KOTLIN STATUS:
- Folder:
- Production use:
- Build use:
- Runtime use:
- Removed/excluded:
- Remaining Kotlin references:

EXPO BUILD:
- Expo project ID:
- Android package:
- Version:
- Version code:
- Build profile:
- GitHub SHA:

COMMANDS:
1. Command:
   Exit code:
   Result:

TESTS:
- Expo doctor:
- Lint:
- Typecheck:
- Unit:
- Integration:
- Navigation:
- Owner login:
- Owner AI:
- Attachments:
- Feed:
- Build:

APK:
- Filename:
- Direct download URL:
- GitHub release/tag:
- Size:
- SHA-256:
- Build ID:
- Timestamp:

LIVE CONNECTIONS:
- API:
- Chat:
- Landing:
- Supabase:
- AI provider:

FINAL STATUS:
VERIFIED / FAILED / BLOCKED

VERIFIED RULE:

Use VERIFIED only when:

- `expo/` is the only active mobile source
- no Kotlin code participates in the build
- no second app exists
- the complete IVX Expo app is inside the APK
- all production URLs are correct
- production tests pass
- a signed APK exists
- a direct owner-accessible download link returns HTTP 200
- GitHub SHA and APK build SHA match
- no P0/P1 build issue remains

Do not ask whether to continue Kotlin.
The owner decision is final: complete IVX using Expo/React Native only.
